conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else has started to feel like a chore lately?
Cooking. I look at the recipe and just feel like I can't do it. I end up ordering in just to get through the evening.
When you feel like you can't do something simple like cooking, what goes through your mind?
I just think I’m getting too old for this. I used to be so independent, and now I’m starting to wonder if I can keep up.
When you wonder if you can keep up, what does that future look like to you?
It looks like a version of me that I don't recognize. One where I can't even make a simple meal without breaking down. It scares me.
You mentioned breaking down—what does that feel like for you?
It feels like I’m losing control. I cry, usually, because I don't know how to stop it.
When you cry like that, does it feel like it comes out of nowhere or is there usually something that triggers it?
It usually happens when I'm alone, and I realize I'm just sitting there, crying over nothing. It's like my body is just letting go of all the stress I've been carrying around.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It’s just so... humiliating. I used to be the strong one in my family, and now I’m just crying on the couch because I can’t keep up.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
I just feel like I'm disappearing. Like, I used to be a person who had plans and goals, and now I’m just existing.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
After my divorce. It took a while to sink in, but eventually, I realized that I don’t have a future to look forward to.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to find the motivation to do stuff during the day?
Definitely. I just feel so drained. Even the simple things, like going to the gym, seem like too much effort.
You mentioned thinking about things you could have done differently—does that leave you feeling disappointed in yourself?
Yeah, a lot. I keep replaying the breakup in my head, wondering if I should have tried harder to make it work.
With your mind replaying the breakup like that, do you find it hard to focus on other things during the day?
Yeah, I do. I sit at work and just stare at my screen, thinking about what I did wrong.
When you're feeling drained and stuck in those thoughts, do you find yourself getting more irritable or on edge with people?
Yeah, definitely. I just snap at people for no reason, and then I feel terrible about it afterward.
You mentioned feeling terrible after snapping at people—what does that feeling of being terrible about it do to you?
It makes me want to just hide away. I don't want to be that person anymore, so I just try to stay quiet and not be around anyone.
When you say you try to stay quiet and not be around anyone, what does that look like for you on a typical day?
I just stay at home and watch TV. I don't want to see anyone or talk to anyone, so I just hide away.
When you're spending so much time at home, has your appetite changed at all?
No, it's been really off. I just don't have the energy to cook or eat, so I usually just skip dinner.
When you skip dinner, do you find that it makes you feel even more tired or low later on?
Yeah, definitely. It makes me feel like I’m just running on empty, and I don't know how to stop it.
